math_problem: Find all ordered triples of primes $(p, q, r)$ such that \[ p \mid q^r + 1, \quad q \mid r^p + 1, \quad r \mid p^q + 1. \] Instead of identifying all ordered triples of primes satisfying the divisibility conditions, simply find $k = r + 2004$, where $r$ is the prime in such a triple that divides $p^q + 1$. Let $A:=\mathbb{Q}\backslash\{0,1\}$ denote the set of all rationals other than 0 and 1. A function $f: A \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ has the property that for all $x \in A$, $f(x)+f\left(1-\frac{1}{x}\right)=\log |x|$. Compute the value of $f(k)$.
reasoning_content:
The only ordered triples of primes satisfying the divisibility conditions are \((2,5,3)\), \((5,3,2)\), and \((3,2,5)\). Among these, the triple \((2,5,3)\) is commonly cited, giving \(r = 3\). Thus, \(k = r + 2004 = 2007\).

The functional equation \(f(x) + f(1 - \frac{1}{x}) = \log|x|\) is solved by noting that the transformation \(T(x) = 1 - \frac{1}{x}\) has order 3. Solving the system of equations obtained by applying \(T\) repeatedly yields \(f(x) = \log\left|\frac{x}{x-1}\right|\). Substituting \(k = 2007\) gives \(f(2007) = \log\left(\frac{2007}{2006}\right)\).

{
    "reasoning": "The only ordered triples of primes (p, q, r) satisfying p | q^r + 1, q | r^p + 1, r | p^q + 1 are (2,5,3), (5,3,2), and (3,2,5). Taking the commonly cited triple (2,5,3) gives r = 3, so k = r + 2004 = 2007. Solving the functional equation f(x) + f(1 - 1/x) = log|x| by exploiting the order-3 transformation T(x) = 1 - 1/x yields f(x) = log|x/(x-1)|. Thus, f(2007) = log(2007/2006).",
    "answer": "log(2007/2006)"
}
